item.description: New Features - Domain allowlist fields for sandbox network config: Added allowedDomains, deniedDomains, allowManagedDomainsOnly, and allowMachLookup fields to SandboxNetworkConfig, bringing parity with the TypeScript schema and enabling Python SDK users to configure network allowlists with proper ty
- Updated to parity with Claude Code v2.1.123
- Fixed OAuth authentication failing with a 401 retry loop when CLAUDECODEDISABLEEXPERIMENTALBETAS=1 is set

item.title: v1.0.110
item.description: Full Changelog: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/compare/v1...v1.0.110

item.title: v2.1.123
item.description: What's changed - Fixed OAuth authentication failing with a 401 retry loop when CLAUDECODEDISABLEEXPERIMENTALBETAS=1 is set

item.title: Merge pull request #1606 from affaan-m/fix/1598-observer-sigusr1-wait
item.description: fix: retry observer wait after SIGUSR1

item.description: # Conflicts: # docs/fixes/HOOK-FIX-20260421-ADDENDUM.md

item.title: fix: retry observer wait after signal
@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.123
item.description: Use Claude, Anthropic's AI assistant, right from your terminal. Claude can understand your codebase, edit files, run terminal commands, and handle entire workflows for you.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: [minor] resolved — The incident is now resolved. The impact was between 16:25 PT / 23:25 UTC and 16:33 PT / 23:33 UTC.

item.title: Merge pull request #1636 from anthropics/pete/remove-transcript-examples-from-agents
item.description: Replace transcript-style agent examples with prose trigger descriptions

item.title: v1.0.109
item.description: What's Changed docs: pullrequesttarget guidance and base-action trust model by @OctavianGuzu in https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/pull/1250 Full Changelog: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/compare/v1...v1.0.109

item.title: v2.1.122
item.description: What's changed - Added ANTHROPICBEDROCKSERVICETIER environment variable to select a Bedrock service tier (default, flex, or priority), sent as the X-Amzn-Bedrock-Service-Tier header - Pasting a PR URL into the /resume search box now finds the session that created that PR (GitHub, GitHub Enterprise,

item.title:
RT @trq212: we're doing a lot more of this, hunting down some of the most annoying bugs in Claude Code
let me know if you have any white w…
item.description:
RT @trq212: we're doing a lot more of this, hunting down some of the most annoying bugs in Claude Code
let me know if you have any white w…

item.title: Replace transcript-style agent examples with prose trigger descriptions
item.description: Several agent files used <example> blocks containing user: "..." / assistant: "..." turn markers, embedded as \n-escaped strings inside the YAML frontmatter description: field. Replace those with flat prose trigger descriptions in description: and a 'When to invoke' section in the agent body contain

item.title:
RT @ClaudeDevs: In the last four Claude Code CLI releases, we’ve shipped 50+ stability and performance fixes.
Faster resume, stable auth,…
item.description:
RT @ClaudeDevs: In the last four Claude Code CLI releases, we’ve shipped 50+ stability and performance fixes.
Faster resume, stable auth,…

item.title:
In the last four Claude Code CLI releases, we’ve shipped 50+ stability and performance fixes.
Faster resume, stable auth, lower memory, fewer hangs: 🧵
item.description:
In the last four Claude Code CLI releases, we’ve shipped 50+ stability and performance fixes.
Faster resume, stable auth, lower memory, fewer hangs: 🧵

item.title: Claude.ai unavailable and elevated errors on the API
item.description: [major] resolved — This incident has been resolved.

item.title: Merge pull request #1582 from anthropics/morganl/code-modernization-plugin
item.description: Add code-modernization plugin

item.title:
AI has broken my mental model for time estimates. I used to lean on Pareto's 80/20: first 80% of the work takes 20% of the time.
I think the underlying principle is still true, but both ratio and cu
item.description:
AI has broken my mental model for time estimates. I used to lean on Pareto's 80/20: first 80% of the work takes 20% of the time.
I think the underlying principle is still true, but both ratio and curve have moved. Getting to "basically works" is nearly instant, sweating every single last detail st

item.title:
Claude Code can now send push notifications to your phone when a long task finishes or Claude needs your input.
Walk away from the terminal, we'll let you know when it's done. https://t.co/FavNOxThSa
item.description:
Claude Code can now send push notifications to your phone when a long task finishes or Claude needs your input.
Walk away from the terminal, we'll let you know when it's done. https://t.co/FavNOxThSa

item.title:
Claude now connects to the tools creative professionals already use.
With the new Blender connector, you can debug a scene, build new tools, or batch-apply changes across every object, directly from
item.description:
Claude now connects to the tools creative professionals already use.
With the new Blender connector, you can debug a scene, build new tools, or batch-apply changes across every object, directly from Claude. https://t.co/Kc3cBHTNpV

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.5
item.description: [minor] resolved — The incident has now been resolved. The timeline for the elevated errors was 6:22 PT / 13:22 UTC -> 6:39 PT / 13:39 UTC.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: [minor] resolved — The incident has now been resolved. The elevated error rate for Haiku 4.5 was between 4:53 PT / 11:53 UTC and 5:36 PT / 12:44 UTC.

item.title: Add adobe-for-creativity plugin (#1625)
item.description: Adobe's Creative Cloud skills for image editing, design automation, background removal, vectorization, and retouching. Points at the plugins/creative-cloud/adobe-for-creativity subdir of adobe/skills.

item.title: Merge pull request #1621 from anthropics/fix/validate-frontmatter-shell-injection
item.description: Harden validate-frontmatter workflow
Documentation - Added docstrings to ClaudeAgentOptions fields for improved IDE autocompletion and inline documentation (873) Internal/Other Changes - Updated bundled Claude CLI to version 2.1.121
- Added updatedToolOutput to PostToolUseHookSpecificOutput for replacing tool output on all tools. updatedMCPToolOutput is deprecated.

- Added alwaysLoad option to MCP server config — when true, all tools from that server skip tool-search deferral and are always available - Added claude plugin prune to remove orphaned auto-installed plugin dependencies; plugin uninstall --prune cascades - Added a type-to-filter search box to /skill

item.title: Claude Code Code Review was intermittently failing
item.description: [none] resolved — Claude Code Code Review intermittently wasn't starting sessions.

item.title: mcp-server-dev: hosting, payload-cap, lifecycle, and directory guidance (#1566)
Fix shell injection in validate-frontmatter workflow
item.description: The 'Validate frontmatter' step interpolated step output directly into a double-quoted shell string, allowing a fork PR that adds a file named e.g. agents/$(curl ...).md to execute arbitrary commands on the runner. - Pass the file list via env: and reference as "$FILES" so the shell never re-eval

item.title: v1.0.108
item.description: Full Changelog: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/compare/v1...v1.0.108

item.title: v2.1.121
item.description: What's changed - Added alwaysLoad option to MCP server config — when true, all tools from that server skip tool-search deferral and are always available - Added claude plugin prune to remove orphaned auto-installed plugin dependencies; plugin uninstall --prune cascades - Added a type-to-filter searc

item.title: Merge pull request #573 from anthropics/claude/copy-memory-cookbook-pr-ZRx6N
item.description: feat(managed_agents): add Managed Agents Memory Cookbook

item.title: @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.121
item.description: Use Claude, Anthropic's AI assistant, right from your terminal. Claude can understand your codebase, edit files, run terminal commands, and handle entire workflows for you.

item.title: Elevated billing related errors on Claude.ai
item.description: [none] resolved — This incident has been resolved.

item.title: fix: yaml.github-actions.security.run-shell-injection.run-shell-injection security vulnerability (#43824)
item.description: Automated security fix generated by Orbis Security AI Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <ubuntu@ip-172-31-32-15.us-west-2.compute.internal>

item.title: Anthropic names Theo Hourmouzis General Manager of Australia & New Zealand and officially opens Sydney office
fix(managed_agents): round 5 of memory cookbook review feedback
item.description: - Simplify status_idle handler to a single unconditional break - Document run_turn return value in the docstring - Restore inline 'Optional: run before cleanup' hint in the seeding cell - Add catalog store cleanup reminder to the combine-stores snippet - Tighten registry description and intro phrasi

item.title: fix(managed_agents): address remaining memory cookbook review items
item.description: - Add wait_for_idle_status calls before session archive in cleanup - Break out of run_turn on any status_idle, not just end_turn - Guard event.input against None in tool_use handler - Move seeding-order warning to a markdown Note callout - Add %pip install cell for minimum SDK version - Link to sibl

item.title: fix(managed_agents): pin agent version in combine-stores snippet too
item.description: The illustrative markdown snippet under "Combine per-customer and shared stores" still passed agent=agent.id while the runnable cells were already using the pinned dict form. Align the snippet so readers copying the production pattern get the same version-pinned shape. https://claude.ai/code/sessio

item.title: fix(managed_agents): pin agent version on sessions.create in memory cookbook
item.description: Pass agent={"type": "agent", "id": agent.id, "version": agent.version} instead of the bare agent.id, matching every other notebook in the managed_agents series. https://claude.ai/code/session_01VkfVPdiJTWVh6Vj5owqRXH

item.title: fix(managed_agents): round 3 of memory cookbook review feedback
item.description: - run_turn: handle session.status_terminated and call wait_for_idle_status before returning, mirroring utilities.stream_until_end_turn; drop the now-redundant wait_for_idle_status calls from the cleanup cell - environments.create: pass an explicit cloud/limited config - agent_toolset_20260401:

item.title: fix(managed_agents): align memory cookbook with CMA series conventions
item.description: - Guard the session.status_idle break on stop_reason.type == "end_turn" so the pattern is safe to copy into custom-tool agents - Import wait_for_idle_status from utilities and call it before each sessions.archive() to absorb the SSE-vs-status race - Use environments.archive instead of environmen

item.title: docs(managed_agents): address review feedback on memory cookbook
item.description: - Prefer uv add over pip in the prerequisites - Clarify that the agent_toolset_20260401 type string identifies the toolset, not a model, and does not need bumping - Drop unused reply variable from the second run_turn call Intentionally skip the dotenv suggestion: the other managed_agents notebook

item.title: feat(managed_agents): add memory store cookbook
item.description: A guided tutorial showing how to give a Managed Agent a memory store so it learns and recalls a customer's shopping preferences across separate sessions. Covers store creation, the resources attachment with per-attachment instructions, inspecting and seeding memories from your own application, and c

item.title: Poisoning Fine-tuning Datasets of Constitutional Classifiers
item.description: We study the conditions needed for a backdoor, installed in a constitutional classifier via fine-tuning data poisoning, to evade black-box red-teaming.

item.title: Introspection Adapters: Training LLMs to Report Their Learned Behaviors
item.description: We introduce introspection adapters (IA), a technique for training an LLM to self-report behaviors it learned during fine-tuning. This IA generalizes to models that were fine-tuned in very different ways.
